reviews.review_msg: Disorganized and a little forgetful. Inconsistent use of email and Canvas was kind of a mess, but otherwise an all right, caring instructor. Lectures were not exciting and contained a lot of unnecessary information that could have been cut to save at least 30 minutes a week. Did learn some useful stuff though so there's that.

Tests, assignments are fair and clear on grading criteria. He is super prompt in replying emails, approachable and nice. Practical questions/assignments (using R) from the beginning to the end of the course. Super helpful

Professor Davis changed my feeling about stats at SFU. After his class I feel very confident in my ability to use the basics of R especially in regression. Tests are very similar to the extensive practice materials. He was always willing to answer any questions in class or in the stats workshop. A great class and great prof!
Jack is great! he is clear and fair. Really recommend taking Stats with him.

Lecture were interesting and you learned something. However, he comes across either as very lazy or disorganized because we had about 15 assignments and didnt get one single grade back. Overall Id prefer profs who are more organized.

Midterms were both fair - because you're allowed a cheat sheet Final exam was a bit harder than midterms but overall fair - because you're allowed a cheat sheet Assignments were super long with some tricky questions Funny sense of humour, never went to lectures even though they probably would've helped
Boring and you didn't really need to know much SAS to do well. If you're looking for an easy class this is the one though

Best prof hands down. Stats can easily be boring and dry but his teaching method makes the course interesting and clear. Good amount of assignments as the exams are almost identical. Would take his class again.
Best stats prof! Exams are just like his practice, only have to read notes and the class is an easy A
